true at iter-1 HEAD but the parity was itself the fail-open (inline comments counted as grants); fixed by making the strip deliberately stricter and re-documenting the divergence

checklist.claim: Each ROOTS workflow grants every REQUIRED_HELPER_HEADS literal so the live-tree assertion holds
checklist.evidence: grant-sync exits 0 on the live tree; re-verified post-fix

checklist.claim: Exempting the sanctioned wildcard cannot open a silent widening path
checklist.evidence: exemption is a one-literal frozenset coupled to a live-grant existence pin; a new wildcard needs a manifest edit plus the review-boundary lock diff

checklist.claim: _ANY_BASH_GRANT_RE enumerates EVERY Bash(...) grant so no widened class escapes
checklist.evidence: enumeration was complete but the CONSUMER dropped covering specs; fixed via _grant_covers

checklist.claim: _classify_widening returns None only for specs that are not widened helper classes
checklist.evidence: falsified: ./scripts/x, ../scripts/x, ~/scripts/x, **/x, *x, bare x all returned None and were waved through; classifier now never returns None

checklist.claim: Basename-only matching is the criterion linking a grant to a reachable helper
checklist.evidence: basename-only keying is blind to directory/blanket globs whose basename is '*'; replaced by the glob-coverage UNION basename-coverage test

phase3_findings.description: _classify_widening fails open on widening shapes it does not enumerate: it recognizes exactly three prefixes and returns None otherwise, and the caller treats None as 'not a widening'. ./scripts/x, ../scripts/x, ~/scripts/x, **/x, *x and bare x are silently accepted for a reachable helper.

Directory-wildcard widening undetected when the tight grant is also present: detection keys on basename membership, so Bash(.devflow/vendor/devflow/scripts/*:*) or Bash(*:*) has basename '*', never a reachable basename, and is skipped — the case AC9 most exists to reject.

Inline-comment blindness: _scan_grants drops only full-line comments, so a grant appearing after code on a line is counted as a real grant and the 'every reachable literal is granted' half passes on an ungranted helper (the silent matcher-denial class).
_grant_source_text catches only OSError; UnicodeDecodeError is a ValueError and escapes as a raw traceback, defeating the documented unknown-is-not-zero contract.
Uncovered arms: _classify_widening's None arm, _grant_source_text's on-disk OSError arm and the missing-key arm, and main(['grant-sync'])'s failure path are all untested.
Test (a) asserts only that some error contains 'grants no explicit' without asserting the error names the dropped literal, and the index slice depends on an unasserted >=2-entry precondition.
Arm (1) duplicates the runtime validator's class 17 (HEAD_ABSENT) without saying so; a future reader may retire one believing the other is sole owner.
